finding_results.evidence: Negative litanies reduced from 9 to 2. Retained: chapters/chapter_14.md:59 ('not optimized, not scheduled, not a prompt requiring confirmation') and chapters/chapter_14.md:105 ('Not because she'd been deemed an unfit parent...'). Replaced with positive assertion at chapters/chapter_14.md:63 ('Her attention was wholly private — her slowness, her incomprehension, the two pages she'd skipped, all of it belonging only to her, unrecorded, vanishing the moment she stood up'), chapters/chapter_14.md:67 ('Sensory data that stayed sensory — just the world, being the world, against the surface of her body'), with sensory detail at chapters/chapter_14.md:167 (Dan's warmth through upholstery), and with sensory ending at chapters/chapter_14.md:209 (car music mixing with Dan's humming). Lines 89, 195, 205, 209 litanies all removed. Final page (lines 193-209) contains zero negative litanies.
finding_results.notes: Two strongest deployments retained: line 59 (non-optimized reading) and line 105 (systemic analysis of school portal loss). Seven removed, with at least four replaced by positive assertion or sensory detail per acceptance test.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_14.md:161 now reads: 'She'd spent weeks guessing what he'd tried. Now the guess was just a fact. The system had absorbed him. The system had restricted her. The methods were different but the result was identical.' Compare to chapters/chapter_14.md:49: 'She didn't know the shape of his rebellion, but she knew the shape of its failure, because the shape was the same shape as every morning that followed.' Maximum consecutive shared words: 2 ('She didn't'). The 'shape of rebellion/failure' formulation is entirely absent from line 161.
finding_results.notes: Register shift from speculative/discursive (line 49) to compressed/final (line 161). The dramatic shift from speculation to confirmation is carried by 'She'd spent weeks guessing what he'd tried. Now the guess was just a fact.' — the collapse from guessing to knowing, rendered as a short declarative.

finding_results.evidence: Distance elaboration reduced to one passage at chapters/chapter_14.md:167 (sensory warmth detail, not analytical commentary). Second distance elaboration at line 195 cut to a single physical detail ('His hand was still on the back, behind where her head would have rested if she'd leaned back.'). Post-pasta commentary at chapters/chapter_14.md:205 reduced to two sentences. Chapter ending at chapters/chapter_14.md:209 now ends on sensory detail: 'trailing a few seconds of music that mixed with Dan's humming and the rattle of the lid and then was gone.' Retrospective catalogue and narrator-level summary cut entirely.
finding_results.notes: The pasta exchange, Dan's hand position, and the sensory ending now carry their own weight without analytical over-narration. The chapter's final image — outside music briefly mixing with Dan's humming then disappearing — works as both sensory detail and thematic gesture without stating the theme.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_14.md:167: 'She could feel the warmth of him across the gap — the specific post-workday heat of Dan's body radiating through the upholstery, a warmth she'd catalogued across fifteen years of marriage without ever deciding to. Her skin registered it before her mind could intervene.' Two sentences. Sensory detail located in Mira's body (skin, warmth). Involuntary ('before her mind could intervene'). Not followed by negation or dismissal — next paragraph begins with Mira's confession about the coffee shop.
finding_results.notes: The warmth detail complicates the maintained distance without resolving it. Scene's emotional architecture (recognition through shared failure, maintained distance) preserved — the awareness is involuntary and unacted-upon, consistent with the couch scene's restraint.

finding_results.evidence: Three dark-comedy beats added to notification sequence. (1) chapters/chapter_14.md:87: 'Availability review — the phrase had the pristine courtesy of a booking cancellation, as though she were a meeting room that hadn't opened its doors.' Absurdity (human as furniture) and cruelty (dehumanization) in simultaneous register. (2) chapters/chapter_14.md:95: 'a former Director of Corporate Strategy could admire the precision even as it gutted her.' Professional appreciation of the weapon deployed against you — comic and devastating simultaneously. (3) chapters/chapter_14.md:115: 'If she'd been a character in the novel she'd been reading, she'd have found the plot implausible.' Comic recognition of the cause-consequence disproportion (novel-reading vs. parental portal loss) rendered as dark-comic observation rather than strategic analysis.
finding_results.notes: All three beats maintain devastation while finding comedy in the disproportion. The humor makes the cruelty worse, not softer — consistent with the creative_risk_policy's 'wince-then-laugh' target. Mira's analytical voice engages the comedy through her specific professional lens (meeting-room metaphor, copywriting admiration, literary criticism).

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_14.md:135 — Dan's first substantive line now reads '"I mean —" He stopped. "They froze your accounts."' The hedging qualifier 'I mean' arrives reflexively (one of his listed syntax-signature markers) and is cut short by the gravity of the fact, marking the shift from his baseline qualifying register to pressure-driven directness. chapters/chapter_14.md:165 — After acknowledging 'Yours was worse,' Dan's thumb snags the armrest fabric and he says '"The upholstery's going."' This is a concrete physical observation (texture, a domestic surface detail) that reveals his specific perceptual habit under stress: fixating on irrelevant tactile details when the abstract becomes unbearable, per his style-bible stress_tells. chapters/chapter_14.md:159 — The pre-existing stress-tell repetition ('Nothing. Nothing happened. That's what happened.') remains unchanged and continues to function as a third distinctive Dan marker.
finding_results.notes: Two new Dan-specific voice markers added to supplement the existing marker at line 159, giving the couch scene three lines with distinctive Dan idiolect. Edit 1 (line 135) deploys a hedging false start ('I mean —') that gets abandoned — showing Dan's qualifier-first syntax interrupted by emotional weight, audibly marking his shift to the direct register the style bible specifies for genuine upset. Edit 2 (line 165) deploys a concrete physical observation ('The upholstery's going') immediately after an emotional admission, enacting his stress tell of grabbing onto textures and surfaces when the larger situation exceeds his language. Both edits are minimal (no surrounding narration changed) to avoid collateral churn. No continuity details introduced or contradicted; the armrest is part of the established couch in the living room. Contraction levels verified: Dan's 'high' contraction level is honored throughout, and Mira's 'moderate' level is maintained. No example_lines from the style bible appear in the chapter.

finding_results.evidence: Morning-routine openings varied: chapters/chapter_14.md:7 now opens 'The shower ran at 6:15' (environment lead), chapters/chapter_14.md:9 now opens 'Dark pants, a blouse she'd ironed' (object lead). Coffee-shop openings varied: chapters/chapter_14.md:35 now opens 'At the counter she ordered' (prepositional lead), chapters/chapter_14.md:41 now opens 'The last physical book she'd held' (object lead), chapters/chapter_14.md:43 now opens 'Back at the table' (location lead), chapters/chapter_14.md:47 now opens 'Between pages, her mind went to Dan' (prepositional lead), chapters/chapter_14.md:57 now opens 'The book drew her back' (object-as-subject lead), chapters/chapter_14.md:67 now opens 'The coffee shop smelled of more than beans' (environment lead). Surviving 'She/Mira [verb]' paragraph openings in lines 3-75: line 5, 11, 23, 25, 27, 31, 53, 75 — total 8. Maximum consecutive run is 3 (lines 23, 25, 27 — the deliberate left-turn sequence). Non-subject openings in morning-routine and coffee-shop sequences: 8.
finding_results.notes: Changed 8 paragraph openings from 'She [verb]' to varied non-subject constructions (environment, object, location, prepositional phrase). Preserved the deliberate anaphoric cluster at lines 23-27 (refusal, walking Theo, turning left) as the strongest candidate for intentional rhythm. The left-turn echo at lines 27/31 ('She turned left' / 'She walked left') is preserved as thematic anaphora with line 29 breaking consecutiveness.

finding_results.evidence: Surviving instances: chapters/chapter_14.md:25 ('the way he always did') and chapters/chapter_14.md:193 ('the way mothers had always known things') — 2 total. Replacements: chapters/chapter_14.md:65 changed from 'the way she'd offered' to 'as she'd offered'; chapters/chapter_14.md:133 changed from 'processing it the way he processed things — not strategically, the way she would' to 'taking it in — not strategically, as she would have' (restructured syntax); chapters/chapter_14.md:145 changed from 'the way you hear yourself recite a number' to 'rote, unbidden, like reciting a phone number she'd memorized against her will' (simile); chapters/chapter_14.md:151 changed from 'the way she dismissed' to 'the reflexive swat she gave' (metaphor); chapters/chapter_14.md:183 changed from 'the way a quarterly report was legible' to 'as a quarterly report was legible' (comparative 'as'); chapters/chapter_14.md:207 changed from 'the way the refrigerator's click was' to 'absorbed into the room's frequency' (direct statement).
finding_results.notes: Reduced from 8 total instances to 2. The finding identified 6 but the chapter contained 8 (lines 65, 151, and 183 were additional unflagged instances). All were addressed. The three replacements specified by the acceptance test (lines 133, 145, 207) use distinct grammatical shapes: restructured syntax with 'as' clause, simile with 'like', and direct statement respectively.

finding_results.evidence: Rectangle of light: chapters/chapter_14.md:63 ('The rectangle of sun on the floor') and chapters/chapter_14.md:111 ('a long rectangle on the floor') — 2 instances with distinct phrasing. Third instance at line 119 changed to 'the light moved off the floor'; fourth at line 117 changed to 'the light on the floor'. Refrigerator click: chapters/chapter_14.md:11 ('The refrigerator clicking its forty-minute click') and chapters/chapter_14.md:111 ('The refrigerator clicking') — 2 instances with distinct phrasing. Third instance at line 147 removed. 'Specific' as emphatic adjective modifying abstract noun: chapters/chapter_14.md:111 changed from 'specific stillness' to 'held-breath stillness'; chapters/chapter_14.md:151 changed from 'The specific, unperformable recognition' to 'The unperformable recognition'. Surviving 'specific' instances at lines 65 and 167 modify physical nouns (heat), not abstract nouns.
finding_results.notes: All three motifs brought to or below the 2-instance cap. Rectangle-of-light had 4 instances (finding identified 3; line 117 was an additional unflagged occurrence). Refrigerator click reduced from 3 to 2 by removing the line-147 instance. 'Specific' as emphatic modifier of abstract nouns reduced from 2 to 0; the two strongest 'specific' uses (line 65 modifying sensory 'heat', line 167 modifying physical 'heat') are retained as they target physical rather than abstract nouns and fall outside the acceptance test's scope.

pass_summaries.summary: All five findings addressed. F14-01 (HIGH): Negative litanies reduced from 9 to 2, with 7 replaced by positive assertions, sensory details, or cuts; final page now contains zero. F14-05: 'Shape of rebellion' echo rewritten as compressed confirmation with no more than 2 consecutive shared words. F14-06: Over-narration trimmed at four locations — distance elaboration consolidated to one sensory passage, post-pasta commentary cut to two sentences, chapter ending now closes on sensory detail (car music + humming) rather than retrospective catalogue. F14-07: Involuntary physical awareness of Dan added at line 167 (his body warmth through the upholstery), two sentences, not negated. F14-08: Three dark-comedy beats integrated into notification sequence, letting Mira's analytical voice find the comedy in institutional language without reducing the devastation. Chapter heading contract preserved. No new continuity contradictions introduced. Character voice and dialogue register verified against style bible.

Single finding F14-09 (MEDIUM) addressed with two surgical dialogue edits. Dan's first substantive line in the couch scene now opens with a hedging false start ('I mean —') that is abandoned under pressure, deploying his syntax-signature qualifying habit. His line at the scene's emotional midpoint now includes a concrete physical observation about the couch fabric, enacting his stress tell of fixating on irrelevant tactile details. Combined with the pre-existing stress-tell repetition at line 159, three of Dan's eleven lines now carry distinctive idiolect markers. The scene's rhythm, Mira's POV narration, leverage shifts, and contraction registers are preserved. No continuity changes, no heading-contract violations, no sanitization.

All three findings resolved. F14-02: Reduced 'She/Mira [verb]' paragraph openings from 16 to 8 in lines 3-75 by introducing environment, object, location, and prepositional leads in morning-routine and coffee-shop sequences. Maximum consecutive run is 3 (the deliberate left-turn sequence at lines 23-27). F14-03: Reduced 'the way [clause]' from 8 instances to 2, retaining lines 25 and 193 as the strongest deployments. Replacements use varied comparative constructions (restructured syntax, simile, metaphor, direct statement). F14-04: All three overused motifs capped at 2 instances each — rectangle-of-light (lines 63, 111), refrigerator-click (lines 11, 111), 'specific' + abstract noun (0 remaining; physical-noun uses preserved). Surviving pairs use distinct phrasing. No sanitization introduced; all revisions maintain or strengthen the chapter's prose texture without softening register or retreating from difficult material.

summary: Merged three-pass revision report (FIXED=9, PARTIAL=0, UNRESOLVED=0).
